text: In a two-phase sanctions proceeding, the U.S. District Court for the District of Oregon struck plaintiff's summary-judgment briefs, dismissed claims with prejudice, and imposed sanctions on pro hac vice counsel Stephen Brigandi for submitting briefing containing non-existent cases and fabricated quotations that the court attributed to an unidentified AI tool. A December 2025 order imposed a $15,500 Rule 11 sanction; the March 2026 order finalized a fee award of $94,704.38 apportioned 85% to Brigandi and 15% to local counsel Timothy Murphy.
deployer: Stephen Brigandi (pro hac vice lead counsel)
canonical_source_conflicted: Non-existent case citations and fabricated quotations; Fed. R. Civ. P. 11
mitigation_gap: Lead counsel submitted AI-generated briefing without verification; court found "one of the clients may have played a role in generating the fake citations" via an unidentified AI tool.
reliance_or_harm: $15,500 monetary sanction on Brigandi; $94,704.38 attorney-fee award ($80,498.72 Brigandi, $14,205.66 Murphy); claims dismissed with prejudice; summary-judgment briefs stricken.
